relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: The community is invited to abound still more in a walk pleasing to the Divine.
The instructions come through Lord Jesus.
Divine will moves toward sanctification and away from misuse.
The body is held in holiness and honor.
Desire is not to rule like an unknowing pattern.
No sibling is to be exploited in the matter.
The call is holiness, not uncleanness.
Rejecting this rejects the Divine who gives holy Spirit.
Love already extends through Macedonia and is invited to abound.
Quiet faithfulness, attending to one's work, and honest labor form peace.
Grief is held differently when hope is present.
Because Jesus died and rose, those who sleep are not lost.
The living do not outrun those who have slept.
The Lord's arrival awakens the dead in Messiah first.
Living and sleeping ones are gathered together to be with the Lord.
These words are given for comfort.

contemplative_note: 1 Thessalonians 4 is holiness and hope: bodies honored, siblings protected, love abounding, grief held by resurrection, and comfort shared.
